title: "Changing DNS on 18.04"
date: 2018-10-10
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless

### Post by leonbrag on 2018-10-10
On 18.04 it appears I should not edit resolver file by hand:

```
leonbrag@minlin:/etc$ cat resolv.conf
# Dynamic resolv.conf(5) file for glibc resolver(3) generated by resolvconf(8)
#     DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN
# 127.0.0.53 is the systemd-resolved stub resolver.
# run "systemd-resolve --status" to see details about the actual nameservers.


nameserver 127.0.0.1
search home.lan



```

So it seems that correct way is setting DNS via netplal YAML config file. But how do I configure YAML to set DNS from DHCP on specific interface (not just a direct address of the DNS server)

thanks

---

### Post by chili555 on 2018-10-10
> So it seems that correct way is setting DNS via netplal YAML config file. If you are running a server; but then, if you are running a server, an IP by DHCP is cumbersome.

If you are runnng a desktop, then setting DNS is easy in Network Manager. [https://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2402695&highlight=DNS](https://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2402695&highlight=DNS)
